macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Indian Markets Plunge on US-Iran War Escalation
items.summary: Nifty 50 opened down 0.80% at 23,674.85 and later fell 1.15% to 23,592; Sensex down 0.64% initially and over 900 points or 1.19% amid ongoing US-Iran conflict and rising oil prices to $100 Brent crude. FIIs sold Rs 6,267 crore while DIIs bought Rs 4,966 crore.

items.title: US Stocks Mixed Amid Iran Conflict and Oil Rally
items.summary: S&P 500 closed down 0.08% at 6,775.80, Dow fell 0.61% to 47,417.27, Nasdaq up 0.08% at 22,716.13 as markets stayed calm despite rising oil prices; investors focused on US-Israeli war on Iran over mild inflation data.
items.source: Bernama / Xinhua / AP via Times Leader

items.title: Asian Markets Open Lower on Geopolitical Tensions
items.summary: Hang Seng down 0.69%, Nikkei 1.16%, ASX 200 1.36%, Shanghai flat at -0.01%, Korea -0.75%, Straits Times -0.33% as oil prices climbed despite strategic reserve releases amid US-Iran hostilities.

items.title: European DAX Drops 1.37% Following Asian Declines
items.summary: Germany's DAX closed down 1.37% at 23,640.03, extending global selloff driven by volatile week, private credit stress, and renewed oil price rally linked to Iran conflict.

items.title: Oracle Surges on Strong Earnings, Campbell's Sinks
items.summary: Oracle jumped 9.2% after beating profit/revenue expectations and raising forecasts due to AI cloud demand; Campbell’s fell 7.1% on weak quarterly profit, snack struggles, and cut guidance.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Wells Fargo Files Crypto Trademark for WFUSD
items.summary: Wells Fargo has filed a crypto-related trademark application for WFUSD, indicating a major push into cryptocurrency and blockchain services. This development signals traditional finance's deepening involvement in crypto.

items.title: Mastercard Launches New Crypto Partner Program
items.summary: Mastercard announced a new Crypto Partner Program to support blockchain adoption. The rollout covers 32 Ethereum Virtual Machine compatible blockchains including Ethereum, BNB Smart Chain, Polygon, and others.

items.title: Ledger Partners with Babylon for Bitcoin Vaults
items.summary: Ledger signers will secure Babylon's trustless Bitcoin vaults, allowing holders to lock BTC into programmable contracts while maintaining self-custody. This enhances Bitcoin's utility in DeFi.

items.title: BlackRock Eyes Major Move into XRP Ledger Tokenization
items.summary: Amid a liquidity crisis in the $2 trillion private credit market, BlackRock is preparing to tokenize securities on the XRP Ledger. Over $2.3 billion in real-world assets have already migrated to the chain in early 2026.

items.title: XRP 2026 Roadmap Unveils Lending Protocol and EVM Sidechain
items.summary: Ripple's 2026 technical roadmap includes XLS-66 native lending protocol and EVM sidechain launch in Q2, expanding XRP Ledger utility beyond payments to yield-generating RWAs.

items.title: Coinbase Expands Crypto-Backed Loans up to $5M
items.summary: Coinbase now offers crypto-backed loans powered by Morpho, allowing users to borrow up to $5 million using BTC or $1 million using ETH as collateral at 4-8% rates.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: L3Harris and Shield AI Achieve Breakthrough in Autonomous Electronic Warfare
items.summary: L3Harris Technologies and Shield AI demonstrated a first-of-its-kind integration of L3Harris' DiSCO electromagnetic battle management with Shield AI’s Hivemind autonomy software, enabling unmanned systems to detect, analyze, and respond to threats in real-time without human intervention.[1]

items.title: Advanced Machine Intelligence (AMI) Launches with $1.03B Funding and Alex LeBrun as CEO
items.summary: New AI research lab AMI launched with $1.03 billion in seed funding, appointing AI entrepreneur Alex LeBrun as CEO to develop 'world models' for real-world understanding beyond generative AI, focusing on persistent memory, reasoning, and safe action planning in sectors like healthcare and robotics.[2]

items.title: MIT Develops Hybrid AI Planner that Turns Images into Robot Action Plans
items.summary: MIT researchers introduced VLMFP, a generative AI framework combining vision-language models with formal solvers to generate long-horizon plans from images for robot tasks, achieving over 80% success on 3D tasks like multirobot collaboration and generalizing to new scenarios.[3]

items.title: University at Buffalo Evaluates AI Models for Wildfire Spread Prediction
items.summary: Researchers tested deep learning models like ConvLSTM against physics-based FARSITE for wildfire forecasting using Hawaiian data, finding AI offers flexibility with real-time data but FARSITE superior in precision and F1 score; AI complements rather than replaces traditional tools.[4]

items.title: AI Autocomplete Features Found to Subtly Influence Views on Social Issues
items.summary: Cornell study in Science Advances shows generative AI autocomplete in writing tasks can bias users' thoughts on topics like the death penalty, with potential to sway public opinion at scale through subtle manipulations.[5]

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Tencent Cloud Unveils AI-Powered Gaming Solutions at GDC 2026
items.summary: Tencent Cloud introduced upgraded Game Multimedia Engagement Solution (GMES) and GVoice technology at the Game Developer's Conference, featuring AI-powered communication, security, and development tools for global gaming platforms including PUBG MOBILE and Delta Force.

items.title: For Honor Celebrates 10th Year with Cycle of War Launch
items.summary: Ubisoft's For Honor launched its 10th year milestone with the Cycle of War update, marking a significant achievement for the live-service game with continued development planned beyond Year 10.

items.title: SAG-AFTRA Issues Do Not Work Order Against Capcom Over Mega Man Game
items.summary: The union issued a directive against Capcom's Mega Man: Dual Override due to the company's failure to begin union signatory procedures, impacting voice actor Ben Diskin and raising concerns about AI protections in non-union contracts.

items.title: PS Plus Game Catalogue for March 2026 Revealed
items.summary: PlayStation Plus announced its March 2026 game catalogue additions, including Persona 5 Royal and Madden NFL 26.

items.title: Iran Attacks Threaten Saudi Arabia's $38 Billion Gaming Initiative
items.summary: Recent Iranian military strikes are impacting investor confidence in Saudi Arabia's Vision 2030 gaming expansion plans, which include the Qiddiya entertainment district and esports infrastructure investments.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Synopsys Launches Ansys 2026 R1 with Digital Twin and Power Integrity Capabilities
items.summary: Synopsys unveiled Ansys 2026 R1 on March 11, 2026, introducing integrated Synopsys-Ansys workflows, digital twin solutions, and Ansys HFSS-PI for advanced 3D power integrity simulation in next-generation chip design and packaging.

items.title: Synopsys Introduces Hardware-Assisted Verification Platforms with 2x Performance Gains
items.summary: Synopsys announced software-defined hardware-assisted verification enhancements on March 11, 2026, delivering up to 2x performance and 2x capacity for ZeBu Server 5 and new HAPS-200 and ZeBu-200 12 FPGA platforms, with ZeBu-200 12 availability scheduled for Q3 2026.

items.title: Synopsys Announces Electronics Digital Twins Platform for System Validation
items.summary: Synopsys unveiled the Electronics Digital Twins (eDT) Platform at Embedded World, enabling end-to-end digital twin foundations to connect silicon designs to software behavior and full-system validation from early development stages.

items.title: Synopsys, AMD, and Microsoft Collaborate on EDA Tools Integration
items.summary: Synopsys, AMD, and Microsoft announced a collaboration to enable faster access to Synopsys EDA tools on Microsoft platforms, powered by AMD compute, supporting co-design of hardware and software for next-generation products.
